title: URL References, Resolution, Identity Keys, Unicode, and Canonicalization · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in URL resolution and identity pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Parse scheme, authority, userinfo, host, port, path, query, and fragment without treating a URL as a trustworthy string.
Resolve relative, scheme-relative, path-relative, query-only, and fragment-only references against the effective document base.
Remove fragments from network-fetch identity while preserving them when the stakeholder report needs document-location evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Unicode Idna Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-unicode-idna-boundary-7y3cps" that uses case 5822 to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fragment handling, conservative fetch identity, and Unicode host evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Unicode Idna Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Unicode Idna Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Unicode Idna Boundary, keep Unicode Idna Boundary connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-unicode-idna-boundary-7y3cps
# Competency: scraper-unicode-idna-boundary.
# Case 5822: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def evidence_scraper_unicode_idna_boundary_7y3cps(references: int = 24, resolved: int = 24, fetch_keys: int = 18, unsafe_merges: int = 0, idna_failures_visible: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_7y3cps = "7y3cps-spc3y7-5822"
    assert evidence_variant_7y3cps
    evidence_axes_7y3cps = (
        "authorization7y3cps",
        "url5822",
        "responsespc3y7",
        "tree7y3cps5822",
        "frontier58227y3cps",
        "reportspc3y75822",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_7y3cps)) == 6
    if references < 1 or resolved < 0:
        return False, resolved, "url-count"
    if resolved != references:
        return False, resolved, "resolution-gap"
    if fetch_keys < 1 or fetch_keys > resolved:
        return False, fetch_keys, "identity-key"
    if unsafe_merges > 0 or idna_failures_visible < 1:
        return False, unsafe_merges, "url-security"
    assert resolved == references and unsafe_merges == 0
    return True, fetch_keys, "conservative-url-identity"
